Die Stelle bleibt offen, bis ein geeigneter Kandidat gefunden wurde. ## Kontakt Die Bewerbung Bewerbungen sollten enthalten: (1) ein Anschreiben, in dem die Bewerber ihre Forschungsinteressen und ihre Motivation für die Bewerbung beschreiben, (2) einen vollständigen Lebenslauf, (3) Kopien relevanter Diplome und Zeugnisse, (4) eine vollständige Liste der Veröffentlichungen mit Details zu den Beiträgen des Kandidaten, (5) mindestens 2 Referenzen (Referenzschreiben sind in dieser Phase nicht erforderlich). Bitte senden Sie Ihre Bewerbung in elektronischer Form oder Anfragen für weitere Informationen an simone.bersini@eoc.ch (bitte verwenden Sie den Betreff: PostDoc position-Vascularized brain). Die Einstellungsbedingungen richten sich nach der Verordnung über die Anstellung von EOC-Personal und Ärzten sowie, für den akademischen Teil, nach dem Statut und der Verordnung des akademischen Personals der USI. Die USI und das EOC streben danach, eine vorbildliche Universität und Arbeitgeberin zu sein und zielen darauf ab, Gleichheit bei der Einstellung, Entwicklung, Bindung und Beförderung von Mitarbeitern zu gewährleisten und sicherzustellen, dass niemand aufgrund von Geschlecht, Kultur, Behinderung, sexueller Orientierung oder Identität benachteiligt wird. Als Institution, die Vielfalt und Inklusion fördert, ermutigt die USI Bewerbungen von Frauen und allen Personen, die unterrepräsentierten Kategorien angehören. Für weitere Informationen kontaktieren Sie bitte: Prof. Simone Bersini, Institut für Translationale Forschung (IRT), Fakultät für Biomedizinische Wissenschaften, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) und Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Schweiz (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Telefon +41 58 666 71 05). Lugano, 20. November 2025 Das betreffende Verfahren unterliegt keinem Rechtsmittel, da der mit dem erfolgreichen Bieter abzuschließende Vertrag auf privatem Recht basiert. Offizielles Ausschreibungsdokument als PDF verfügbar.
requirements: Promotion in Lebenswissenschaften oder verwandten Disziplinen (inkl. Bioingenieurwesen); Bevorzugung Vaskuläre Biologie oder Neurowissenschaften.
Hohes Interesse an Organ-on-a-Chip, Biofabrikation und (Bio-)Printing.
Erfahrung mit 3D-Zellkulturen, vorzugsweise vaskuläre Zellen/Gehirnorganoide.
Erfahrung mit Bildgebung (konfokale Mikroskopie, High-Content-Screening).
Kenntnisse in Standard-Biologietechniken (qPCR, Western Blot, ELISA, Immunfluoreszenz).
Erfahrung in Design/Mikrofabrikation von mikro-physiologischen Systemen.
Fähigkeit zur Analyse komplexer Datensätze (inkl. Omics-Technologien).
Exzellente Englischkenntnisse (mündlich/schriftlich), Motivation, Organisationstalent und Teamfähigkeit/Selbstständigkeit.

fr.title: PostDoc Biofabrication/Biologie Vasculaire - Vascu. Organoïdes Cérébraux
fr.description: ## Description Poste ouvert à l'Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) et à l'Università della Svizzera italiana (USI). Rôle : PostDoc en Biofabrication/Biologie Vasculaire axé sur la vascularisation des organoïdes cérébraux. Lieu : Lugano, Suisse (Canton du Tessin). L'Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) et l'Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) recherchent des candidats pour le poste de PostDoc en Biofabrication/Biologie Vasculaire avec un accent sur la vascularisation des organoïdes cérébraux. La Division de Médecine Régénérative fait partie de l'Institut de Recherche Translationnelle, Faculté des Sciences Biomédicales, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) et Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Suisse. Les domaines de recherche stratégiques de la Division sont : la modélisation de maladies in vitro par biofabrication (ex : maladies liées à l'âge, métastases cancéreuses, maladies musculo-squelettiques) ; la conception de nouvelles technologies pour le criblage de médicaments ; les applications de médecine personnalisée utilisant des biopsies de tissus humains. Pour promouvoir l'avancement de ces domaines de recherche, la Division de Médecine Régénérative combine la microfluidique et les systèmes microphysiologiques, l'impression 3D (bio) et les simulations computationnelles. Nous invitons les candidatures pour un poste de PostDoc afin de mener des recherches dans le cadre du projet intitulé « Enhancing human brain organoid complexity through biofabrication of a perfusable vasculature ». Le Projet La Division a récemment obtenu un financement du Fonds National Suisse de la Recherche grâce à une collaboration entre le Prof. Simone Bersini et le Prof. Arianna Baggiolini (Institut de Recherche en Oncologie). Le projet de 4 ans portera sur la biofabrication de modèles miniaturisés du cerveau humain (c'est-à-dire des organoïdes) et leur intégration avec des réseaux microvasculaires fonctionnels. Cette technologie de pointe permettra d'étudier les mécanismes clés de la différenciation des progéniteurs neuronaux pendant le développement cérébral et de récapituler des architectures de plus en plus complexes du cerveau humain. Le projet implique la biofabrication de réseaux microvasculaires 3D imitant la barrière hémato-encéphalique, leur intégration avec des systèmes de culture à haut débit et l'analyse de l'interaction entre les vaisseaux sanguins et les organoïdes cérébraux par diverses techniques de séquençage de nouvelle génération et de criblage à haut contenu. L'Institut a accès à des installations de pointe (ex : RNAseq bulk et single-cell ; transcriptomique spatiale ; spectrométrie de masse ; microscopie confocale, multiphotonique et électronique) qui sont partagées avec l'Institut de Recherche en Biomédecine et l'Institut de Recherche en Oncologie dans un environnement dynamique, multidisciplinaire et collaboratif. Le Poste de PostDoc Le candidat travaillera sous la supervision scientifique du Prof. Simone Bersini (https://search.usi.ch/en/people/af37e00b27d5199e68bb294f19e50006/bersini-simone). Le candidat retenu aura la possibilité de travailler dans une équipe de recherche dynamique et dans un environnement scientifique multidisciplinaire et international. Le candidat collaborera au développement de l'agenda de recherche de l'institut. Il/elle aura des responsabilités partagées dans la conception, la conduite et l'analyse de projets de recherche dans le domaine de la biofabrication et de la biologie moléculaire cellulaire. De plus, le candidat devra présenter des travaux lors de conférences scientifiques et produire des publications dans des revues de haut niveau. ## Exigences Les candidats idéaux devraient satisfaire aux exigences suivantes : - Un doctorat dans un domaine des sciences de la vie ou disciplines connexes, y compris le bio-ingénierie. Priorité aux candidats ayant une formation en biologie vasculaire ou en neurosciences. - Fort intérêt personnel pour les organ-on-a-chip, la biofabrication et l'impression (bio). - Expérience avec (ou engagement fort à apprendre rapidement) : cultures cellulaires 3D (expérience préférée avec des cellules vasculaires et/ou des organoïdes cérébraux), imagerie (microscopie confocale, de préférence avec des cultures 3D et utilisant des systèmes de criblage à haut contenu), techniques biologiques standard (qPCR, Western blot, ELISA, immunofluorescence), conception et microfabrication de systèmes microphysiologiques, analyse de jeux de données complexes, y compris ceux issus de technologies omiques. - Excellentes compétences en anglais oral et écrit. - Auto-motivation et engagement exceptionnel envers les objectifs et les délais expérimentaux. - Solides compétences organisationnelles et capacité à travailler de manière autonome ainsi qu'en équipe. - Analyse critique des données et résolution de problèmes. - Communiquer efficacement les données expérimentales, tenir des registres et rédiger des manuscrits. ## Ce que nous offrons Conditions contractuelles Le candidat retenu travaillera comme PostDoc au sein de la Division de Médecine Régénérative et aura la possibilité d'interagir avec un réseau international de collaborateurs. Des interactions continues et dynamiques avec le laboratoire du Prof. Baggiolini sont attendues pour atteindre les objectifs du projet. Le lieu de travail est BIOS+, situé à Bellinzone, Suisse. La disponibilité pour voyager dans d'autres régions de Suisse et à l'étranger (à des fins de collaboration et de recherche) est requise. La date de début est le 1er mars 2026. Le poste restera ouvert jusqu'à ce qu'un candidat approprié soit trouvé. ## Contact La candidature Les candidatures doivent contenir : (1) une lettre décrivant les intérêts de recherche et la motivation du candidat, (2) un CV complet, (3) des copies des diplômes et certificats pertinents, (4) une liste complète des publications avec des détails sur les contributions du candidat, (5) un minimum de 2 références (lettres de recommandation non requises à ce stade). Veuillez envoyer votre candidature sous forme électronique ou vos demandes d'informations supplémentaires à simone.bersini@eoc.ch (veuillez utiliser l'objet suivant : PostDoc position-Vascularized brain). Les conditions d'engagement sont celles prévues par le Règlement concernant l'engagement du personnel de l'EOC et des médecins ainsi que, pour la partie académique, par le Statut et le Règlement du personnel académique de l'USI. L'USI et l'EOC aspirent à être une université et un employeur exemplaires et visent à garantir l'égalité dans l'embauche, le développement, la rétention et la promotion du personnel, et à ce que personne ne soit désavantagé en raison de son sexe, de sa culture, de son handicap, de son orientation sexuelle ou de son identité. En tant qu'institution qui promeut la diversité et l'inclusion, l'USI encourage les candidatures des femmes et de toutes les personnes appartenant à des catégories sous-représentées. Pour plus d'informations, veuillez contacter : Prof. Simone Bersini, Institut de Recherche Translationnelle (IRT), Faculté des Sciences Biomédicales, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) et Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Suisse (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Téléphone +41 58 666 71 05). Lugano, le 20 novembre 2025 La procédure en question n'est soumise à aucun moyen de recours ou de voie de droit, le contrat à conclure avec le soumissionnaire retenu étant basé sur le droit privé. Appel d'offres officiel disponible en PDF.
requirements: Doctorat en Sciences de la Vie ou disciplines connexes (Bioingénierie incluse) ; priorité à la Biologie Vasculaire ou aux Neurosciences.
Fort intérêt pour les organ-on-a-chip, la biofabrication et l'impression (bio).
Expérience en cultures cellulaires 3D, idéalement cellules vasculaires/organoïdes cérébraux.
Expérience en imagerie (microscopie confocale, criblage à haut contenu).
Maîtrise des techniques biologiques standard (qPCR, Western blot, ELISA, immunofluorescence).
Expérience en conception/microfabrication de systèmes microphysiologiques.
Capacité d'analyse de données complexes (y compris technologies omiques).
Excellentes compétences en anglais (oral/écrit), motivation, organisation et capacité à travailler en équipe/autonomie.

entries.value:
## Descrizione

Posizione aperta presso Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) and Università della Svizzera italiana (USI).

Ruolo: PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization.

Sede: Lugano, Svizzera (Canton Ticino).

The Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) and the Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) are seeking applications for the position of PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization The Regenerative Medicine Division is part of the Institute for Translational Research, Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland. Strategic research areas of the Division are: in vitro disease modeling through biofabrication (e.g. age- related diseases, cancer metastases, musculo-skeletal diseases); design of novel technologies for drug screening; personalized medicine applications using human tissue biopsies. To promote the advancement of these research areas, the Regenerative Medicine Division combines microfluidics and microphysiological systems, 3D (bio)printing and computational simulations. We invite applications for a PhD position for conducting research in the project entitled “Enhancing human brain organoid complexity through biofabrication of a perfusable vasculature”.

The project The Division has recently granted funding from the Swiss National Science Foundation through a collaboration between Prof. Simone Bersini and Prof. Arianna Baggiolini (Institute of Oncology Research). The 4 years project will deal with the biofabrication of miniaturized models of the human brain (i.e. organoids) and their integration with functional microvascular networks. This cutting-edge technology will allow to study key mechanisms of neural progenitor cell differentiation during brain development and to recapitulate increasingly complex architectures of the human brain. The project involves the biofabrication of 3D microvascular networks mimicking the blood brain barrier, their integration with high-throughput culture systems and the analysis of blood vessel-brain organoid interaction through multiple next-gen sequencing techniques and high-content screening. The Institute has access to cutting-edge facilities (e.g. bulk and single-cell RNAseq; spatial transcriptomics; mass spectrometry; confocal, multi-photon and electron microscopy) which are shared with the Institute for Research in Biomedicine and the Institute of Oncology Research within a dynamic, multidisciplinary and collaborative environment.

The PhD Position The candidate will work under the scientific supervision of Prof. Simone Bersini (https://search.usi.ch/en/people/af37e00b27d5199e68bb294f19e50006/bersini-simone). The successful candidate will be offered the possibility to work in a dynamic research team and in a multidisciplinary and international scientific environment. He or she will be enrolled in the Doctoral Program in Biomedical Sciences at USI. The candidate will collaborate in the development of the institute research agenda. He or she will have shared responsibilities in the design, conduction and analysis of research projects in the field of biofabrication and molecular cell biology. In addition, the candidate is expected to present papers at scientific conferences and produce publications in high-ranking journals.

## Requisiti

Candidates’ profile Ideal candidates should satisfy the following requirements:
- A Master Degree in any Life Sciences or related disciplines, including Bioengineering. Priority given to Vascular Biology or Neuroscience background
- High personal interest in organ-on-a-chip, biofabrication and (bio)-printing
- Experience with (or strong commitment to quickly learn): 3D cell cultures (preferred experience 2 with vascular cells and/or brain organoids), imaging (confocal microscopy, preferably with 3D cultures), standard biological techniques (qPCR, western blot, elisa, immunofluorescence), microfabrication of microphysiological systems, analysis of datasets including those from omic technologies
- Excellent skills in oral and written English
- Self-motivation and exceptional commitment to experimental goals and deadlines
- Strong organizational skills and ability to work independently as well as in a team
- Critical data analysis and troubleshooting
- Effectively communicate experimental data, maintain records and write protocols

## Cosa offriamo

Contract terms The successful candidate will work as a PhD student in the Regenerative Medicine Division, and will have the possibility to interact with an international network of collaborators. Continuous and dynamic interactions with the lab of Prof. Baggiolini are expected to achieve the goals of the project. Workplace is BIOS+, located in Bellinzona, Switzerland. Availability to travel to other parts of Switzerland and abroad (for purposes of collaboration and research) is required. Starting date is March 1, 2026. The position will be kept open until a suitable candidate has been found.

## Candidatura

The application Applications should contain:
(1) a letter in which the applicants describe their research interests and the motivation to apply,
(2) a complete CV,
(3) copies of relevant diplomas and certificates,
(4) a complete list of publications with details on the candidate’s contributions,
(5) minimum 2 references (letters not required at this stage).

Please send your application in electronic form or requests for further information to simone.bersini@eoc.ch (please use the following subject: PhD position-Vascularized brain)

The conditions of appointment are those provided for by the Regulation concerning the hiring of EOC staff and doctors as well as, for the academic part, by the Statute and Regulations of USI academic staff. USI and the EOC aspire to be an exemplary university and employer and aim to ensure equality in the hiring, development, retention and promotion of staff, and that no one is disadvantaged on the basis of gender, culture, disability, sexual orientation or identity. As an institution that promotes diversity and inclusion, USI encourages applications from women and all people belonging to underrepresented categories.

For further information, please contact:
Prof. Simone Bersini, Institute for Translational Research (IRT), Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Phone +41 58 666 71 05).

Lugano, November 19, 2025

The procedure in question is not subject to any means of appeal and/or remedy in law, since the contract to be concluded with the successful bidder is based on private law

Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.

en.title: Two Post-Doctoral Researchers
en.description: ## Descrizione Posizione aperta presso USI – Università della Svizzera italiana. Dipartimento/Istituto: Facoltà di scienze economiche – Institute of Management and Organisation (IMO). Ruolo: Two Post-Doctoral Researchers. Sede: Lugano, Svizzera (Canton Ticino). 1 Institute of Management and Organization (IMO) Two Post-Doctoral Researchers in Management at the Institute of Management & Organization (IMO), Faculty of Economics, at USI Università della Svizzera italiana Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) is a young and lively university, a hub of opportunity open to the world where students are offered a quality interdisciplinary education in which they can be fully engaged and take center stage, and where our researchers can count on having the space to freely pursue their initiative. Established in 1996, USI is in constant evolution, always taking on new challenges while remaining true to its three guiding principles: quality, openness, and responsibility. The director of the Humanitarian Operations Group, Prof. Paulo Goncalves, invites applications for two post-doctoral researcher positions to work on a 4-year EU-HORIZON project seeking to develop an European Pandemic Preparedness System. ## The Post-Doctoral Researcher Position The post-doctoral researcher will work under the scientific supervision of Prof. Paulo Goncalves (http://usi.to/iam). The successful candidates will be offered the possibility to work in a dynamic research team and in a multidisciplinary and international scientific environment. The post-doctoral researchers will work on an EU-HORIZON project seeking to develop an European Pandemic Preparedness System. The project is supported in Switzerland by the State Secretariat for Education, Research, and Innovation (SERI). The post-doctoral researchers will be developing a system dynamics model to assess health emergency threat and different countries’ immediate response capability, identifying risks, ensuring planning, and prioritizing health emergency preparedness activities. They will also: - Collect and clean quantitative relevant European data for the dynamic model. - Plan and conduct stakeholder engagement activities, such as participatory systems modelling workshops, semi-structured interviews, and surveys. - Contribute to model development in collaboration with research partners. - Develop SD model structure, calibrate the model to data, and design and test health response policies. - Design and implement a management flight simulator for the dynamic threat assessment of health emergencies. - Publish in high-quality peer-reviewed international journals and to present research findings at national and international meetings and scientific conferences. - Undertake any necessary training and/or development. - Undertake all appropriate administrative tasks. ## Candidates’ profile The ideal candidates must meet the following requirements: - Hold a PhD in system dynamics, simulation modeling, or a related field, and equivalent 2 research experience. - Experience with system dynamics models for public health - Experience with model-based and data-driven policy analysis. - Experience with quantitative data collection, analysis, and management in a multi-disciplinary environment (e.g., econometrics, optimization, simulation) - Experience with social engagement processes (e.g., participatory systems modelling, semi- structured interviews, workshops, focus groups). - Excellent oral and written English, the official language for the research; understanding of or interest in learning Italian is welcome. - Experience with publication in international peer-reviewed journals. - Ability to work independently and autonomously, to plan, direct, and make progress on own research. - High personal interest in public health and health assessment models - Willingness to travel both within Switzerland and abroad to conduct research and attend conferences. ## Contract terms The post-doctoral research positions are highly competitive. Hiring decisions are based on the candidate’s background, interests, attitude, and potential for academic achievement. The position requires full-time commitment and is not compatible with further professional activity. The successful candidates will work under the supervision of Professor Gonçalves on an EU- HORIZON project to develop an European Pandemic Preparedness System funded by 4-years. The post-doctoral researchers will have the possibility to collaborate and interact with a team of other doctoral and post-doctoral researchers working on other research projects, as well as an international network of collaborators. Workplace is USI Università della Svizzera italiana, located in Lugano, Switzerland. Availability to travel to other parts of Switzerland and abroad (for purposes of collaboration and research) is required. Starting date is as soon as possible. ## The Application Applications should contain: (1) A personal statement in which the applicant describes their research interests and the motivation to apply, (2) A complete CV, including (i) date of birth, (ii) nationality, (iii) names of two references (see p.3), and (iv) other information relevant for the position (e.g., system dynamics modeling). (3) Names and contact information (e.g., email and telephone) of two professional references supporting your application. (4) An electronic version of research work (scientific published articles in peer-reviewed international journals), accompanied by a short research summary in English (1 page maximum). The application deadline is March 30, 2024. Please send your application in electronic form in a single PDF file directly to paulo.goncalves@usi.ch (also requests for further information). Due to the large number of applications received, only those applicants whose profiles are likely to fit the positions will be notified for further details and eventual interviews. We will start interviewing qualified applicants shortly after the submission deadline. As an institution that values diversity, USI particularly encourages applications from women and from all individuals from underrepresented groups. Lugano, March 4 th, 2024 Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.

it.title: Due Ricercatori Post-Dottorato
it.description: ## Descrizione Posizione aperta presso USI – Università della Svizzera italiana. Dipartimento/Istituto: Facoltà di scienze economiche – Institute of Management and Organisation (IMO). Ruolo: Due Ricercatori Post-Dottorato. Sede: Lugano, Svizzera (Canton Ticino). 1 Institute of Management and Organization (IMO) Due Ricercatori Post-Dottorato in Management presso l'Institute of Management & Organization (IMO), Facoltà di Economia, presso USI Università della Svizzera italiana Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) è un'università giovane e vivace, un polo di opportunità aperto al mondo dove agli studenti viene offerta un'educazione interdisciplinare di qualità in cui possono essere pienamente coinvolti e al centro della scena, e dove i nostri ricercatori possono contare sullo spazio per perseguire liberamente la propria iniziativa. Fondata nel 1996, USI è in continua evoluzione, affrontando sempre nuove sfide pur rimanendo fedele ai suoi tre principi guida: qualità, apertura e responsabilità. Il direttore del Humanitarian Operations Group, Prof. Paulo Goncalves, invita a presentare candidature per due posizioni di ricercatore post-dottorato per lavorare su un progetto EU-HORIZON di 4 anni volto a sviluppare un Sistema Europeo di Preparazione alle Pandemie. ## La Posizione di Ricercatore Post-Dottorato Il ricercatore post-dottorato lavorerà sotto la supervisione scientifica del Prof. Paulo Goncalves (http://usi.to/iam). Ai candidati selezionati verrà offerta la possibilità di lavorare in un team di ricerca dinamico e in un ambiente scientifico multidisciplinare e internazionale. I ricercatori post-dottorato lavoreranno su un progetto EU-HORIZON volto a sviluppare un Sistema Europeo di Preparazione alle Pandemie. Il progetto è supportato in Svizzera dalla Segreteria di Stato per la Formazione, la Ricerca e l'Innovazione (SERI). I ricercatori post-dottorato svilupperanno un modello di dinamica dei sistemi per valutare la minaccia di emergenze sanitarie e la capacità di risposta immediata dei diversi paesi, identificando i rischi, garantendo la pianificazione e dando priorità alle attività di preparazione alle emergenze sanitarie. Lavoreranno inoltre per: - Raccogliere e pulire dati quantitativi europei pertinenti per il modello dinamico. - Pianificare e condurre attività di coinvolgimento degli stakeholder, come workshop di modellazione partecipativa dei sistemi, interviste semi-strutturate e sondaggi. - Contribuire allo sviluppo del modello in collaborazione con i partner di ricerca. - Sviluppare la struttura del modello SD, calibrare il modello sui dati e progettare e testare politiche di risposta sanitaria. - Progettare e implementare un simulatore di volo gestionale per la valutazione dinamica delle minacce di emergenze sanitarie. - Pubblicare su riviste internazionali peer-reviewed di alta qualità e presentare i risultati della ricerca in incontri nazionali e internazionali e conferenze scientifiche. - Intraprendere qualsiasi formazione e/o sviluppo necessario. - Svolgere tutti i compiti amministrativi appropriati. ## Profilo dei Candidati I candidati ideali devono soddisfare i seguenti requisiti: - Possedere un dottorato di ricerca in dinamica dei sistemi, modellazione di simulazione o un campo correlato, ed esperienza di ricerca equivalente di 2 anni. - Esperienza con modelli di dinamica dei sistemi per la sanità pubblica - Esperienza con analisi di policy basate su modelli e dati. - Esperienza nella raccolta, analisi e gestione di dati quantitativi in un ambiente multidisciplinare (ad es. econometria, ottimizzazione, simulazione) - Esperienza con processi di coinvolgimento sociale (ad es. modellazione partecipativa dei sistemi, interviste semi-strutturate, workshop, focus group). - Eccellente conoscenza dell'inglese orale e scritto, lingua ufficiale della ricerca; la conoscenza o l'interesse ad apprendere l'italiano è gradita. - Esperienza di pubblicazione su riviste internazionali peer-reviewed. - Capacità di lavorare in modo indipendente e autonomo, di pianificare, dirigere e progredire nella propria ricerca. - Alto interesse personale per la sanità pubblica e i modelli di valutazione sanitaria - Disponibilità a viaggiare sia in Svizzera che all'estero per condurre ricerche e partecipare a conferenze. ## Termini del Contratto Le posizioni di ricerca post-dottorato sono altamente competitive. Le decisioni di assunzione si basano sul background, sugli interessi, sull'attitudine e sul potenziale di successo accademico del candidato. La posizione richiede un impegno a tempo pieno e non è compatibile con ulteriori attività professionali. I candidati selezionati lavoreranno sotto la supervisione del Professor Gonçalves su un progetto EU-HORIZON per sviluppare un Sistema Europeo di Preparazione alle Pandemie finanziato per 4 anni. I ricercatori post-dottorato avranno la possibilità di collaborare e interagire con un team di altri ricercatori dottorandi e post-dottorato che lavorano su altri progetti di ricerca, nonché con una rete internazionale di collaboratori. Il luogo di lavoro è USI Università della Svizzera italiana, situata a Lugano, Svizzera. È richiesta la disponibilità a viaggiare in altre parti della Svizzera e all'estero (a fini di collaborazione e ricerca). La data di inizio è il prima possibile. ## La Candidatura Le candidature devono contenere: (1) Una lettera di presentazione personale in cui il candidato descrive i propri interessi di ricerca e la motivazione alla candidatura, (2) Un CV completo, comprensivo di (i) data di nascita, (ii) nazionalità, (iii) nomi di due referenti (vedi p.3), e (iv) altre informazioni pertinenti per la posizione (ad es. modellazione della dinamica dei sistemi). (3) Nomi e informazioni di contatto (ad es. email e telefono) di due referenti professionali a supporto della vostra candidatura. (4) Una versione elettronica del lavoro di ricerca (articoli scientifici pubblicati su riviste internazionali peer-reviewed), accompagnata da un breve riassunto della ricerca in inglese (massimo 1 pagina). La scadenza per la presentazione delle candidature è il 30 marzo 2024. Si prega di inviare la candidatura in formato elettronico in un unico file PDF direttamente a paulo.goncalves@usi.ch (anche richieste di ulteriori informazioni). A causa del gran numero di candidature ricevute, solo i candidati il cui profilo è probabile che corrisponda alle posizioni saranno contattati per ulteriori dettagli ed eventuali colloqui. Inizieremo a intervistare i candidati qualificati poco dopo la scadenza della presentazione. In quanto istituzione che valorizza la diversità, USI incoraggia in particolare le candidature di donne e di tutti gli individui appartenenti a gruppi sottorappresentati. Lugano, 4 marzo 2024 Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.
requirements: Dottorato in dinamica dei sistemi, modellazione di simulazione o campo correlato.
2 anni di esperienza di ricerca equivalente.
Esperienza con modelli di dinamica dei sistemi per la sanità pubblica.
Esperienza con analisi di policy basate su modelli e dati.
Esperienza nella raccolta, analisi e gestione di dati quantitativi.
Esperienza con processi di coinvolgimento sociale.
Eccellente inglese orale e scritto.
Disponibilità a viaggiare.

de.title: Zwei Postdoktoranden
de.description: ## Beschreibung Stellenausschreibung bei USI – Università della Svizzera italiana. Abteilung/Institut: Fakultät für Wirtschaftswissenschaften – Institute of Management and Organisation (IMO). Rolle: Zwei Postdoktoranden. Standort: Lugano, Schweiz (Kanton Tessin). 1 Institute of Management and Organization (IMO) Zwei Postdoktoranden in Management am Institute of Management & Organization (IMO), Fakultät für Wirtschaftswissenschaften, an der USI Università della Svizzera italiana. Die USI Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) ist eine junge und lebendige Universität, ein Zentrum der Möglichkeiten, das der Welt offensteht, wo Studierenden eine qualitativ hochwertige interdisziplinäre Ausbildung angeboten wird, in der sie sich voll engagieren und im Mittelpunkt stehen können, und wo unsere Forscher auf Raum zählen können, um ihre Initiative frei zu verfolgen. Gegründet 1996, befindet sich die USI in ständiger Entwicklung, nimmt immer neue Herausforderungen an und bleibt dabei ihren drei Leitprinzipien treu: Qualität, Offenheit und Verantwortung. Der Direktor der Humanitarian Operations Group, Prof. Paulo Goncalves, lädt zur Bewerbung für zwei Postdoktorandenstellen ein, um an einem 4-jährigen EU-HORIZON-Projekt zur Entwicklung eines europäischen Pandemie-Vorsorgesystems zu arbeiten. ## Die Postdoktorandenstelle Der Postdoktorand wird unter der wissenschaftlichen Betreuung von Prof. Paulo Goncalves (http://usi.to/iam) arbeiten. Den erfolgreichen Kandidaten wird die Möglichkeit geboten, in einem dynamischen Forschungsteam und in einem multidisziplinären und internationalen wissenschaftlichen Umfeld zu arbeiten. Die Postdoktoranden werden an einem EU-HORIZON-Projekt arbeiten, das die Entwicklung eines europäischen Pandemie-Vorsorgesystems zum Ziel hat. Das Projekt wird in der Schweiz vom Staatssekretariat für Bildung, Forschung und Innovation (SBFI) unterstützt. Die Postdoktoranden werden ein Systemdynamikmodell entwickeln, um die Bedrohung durch Gesundheitsnotfälle und die unmittelbare Reaktionsfähigkeit verschiedener Länder zu bewerten, Risiken zu identifizieren, die Planung sicherzustellen und die Vorbereitungsaktivitäten für Gesundheitsnotfälle zu priorisieren. Sie werden außerdem: - Relevante quantitative europäische Daten für das dynamische Modell sammeln und bereinigen. - Stakeholder-Engagement-Aktivitäten planen und durchführen, wie z. B. partizipative Systemmodellierungsworkshops, halbstrukturierte Interviews und Umfragen. - In Zusammenarbeit mit Forschungspartnern zur Modellentwicklung beitragen. - Die SD-Modellstruktur entwickeln, das Modell anhand von Daten kalibrieren und Strategien zur Reaktion auf Gesundheitsnotfälle entwerfen und testen. - Einen Management-Flugsimulator für die dynamische Bedrohungsbewertung von Gesundheitsnotfällen entwerfen und implementieren. - In hochwertigen, begutachteten internationalen Fachzeitschriften veröffentlichen und Forschungsergebnisse auf nationalen und internationalen Tagungen und wissenschaftlichen Konferenzen präsentieren. - Notwendige Schulungen und/oder Weiterbildungen absolvieren. - Alle angemessenen administrativen Aufgaben erledigen. ## Kandidatenprofil Die idealen Kandidaten müssen folgende Anforderungen erfüllen: - Promotion in Systemdynamik, Simulationsmodellierung oder einem verwandten Fachgebiet und gleichwertige 2-jährige Forschungserfahrung. - Erfahrung mit Systemdynamikmodellen für die öffentliche Gesundheit - Erfahrung mit modellbasierten und datengesteuerten Politikanalysen. - Erfahrung in der Sammlung, Analyse und Verwaltung quantitativer Daten in einem multidisziplinären Umfeld (z. B. Ökonometrie, Optimierung, Simulation) - Erfahrung mit Prozessen des sozialen Engagements (z. B. partizipative Systemmodellierung, halbstrukturierte Interviews, Workshops, Fokusgruppen). - Ausgezeichnete mündliche und schriftliche Englischkenntnisse, die offizielle Sprache für die Forschung; Kenntnisse oder Interesse an der italienischen Sprache sind willkommen. - Erfahrung mit Veröffentlichungen in internationalen begutachteten Fachzeitschriften. - Fähigkeit, unabhängig und autonom zu arbeiten, die eigene Forschung zu planen, zu leiten und voranzutreiben. - Hohes persönliches Interesse an Public Health und Gesundheitsbewertungsmodellen - Bereitschaft zu Reisen innerhalb der Schweiz und ins Ausland zur Durchführung von Forschung und Teilnahme an Konferenzen. ## Vertragsbedingungen Die Postdoktorandenstellen sind sehr wettbewerbsintensiv. Einstellungsentscheidungen basieren auf dem Hintergrund, den Interessen, der Einstellung und dem Potenzial für akademische Leistungen des Kandidaten. Die Stelle erfordert eine Vollzeitbeschäftigung und ist mit keiner weiteren beruflichen Tätigkeit vereinbar. Die erfolgreichen Kandidaten arbeiten unter der Aufsicht von Professor Gonçalves an einem EU-HORIZON-Projekt zur Entwicklung eines europäischen Pandemie-Vorsorgesystems, das für 4 Jahre finanziert wird. Die Postdoktoranden haben die Möglichkeit, mit einem Team anderer Doktoranden und Postdoktoranden, die an anderen Forschungsprojekten arbeiten, sowie mit einem internationalen Netzwerk von Kooperationspartnern zusammenzuarbeiten und zu interagieren. Arbeitsort ist die USI Università della Svizzera italiana in Lugano, Schweiz. Reisebereitschaft innerhalb der Schweiz und ins Ausland (zu Kooperations- und Forschungszwecken) ist erforderlich. Beginn ist so bald wie möglich. ## Die Bewerbung Die Bewerbungen sollten enthalten: (1) Ein persönliches Anschreiben, in dem der Bewerber seine Forschungsinteressen und die Motivation für die Bewerbung beschreibt, (2) Einen vollständigen Lebenslauf, einschließlich (i) Geburtsdatum, (ii) Nationalität, (iii) Namen von zwei Referenzen (siehe S. 3) und (iv) anderer für die Stelle relevanter Informationen (z. B. Systemdynamikmodellierung). (3) Namen und Kontaktdaten (z. B. E-Mail und Telefon) von zwei professionellen Referenzen, die Ihre Bewerbung unterstützen. (4) Eine elektronische Version der Forschungsarbeit (wissenschaftliche Artikel, die in internationalen begutachteten Fachzeitschriften veröffentlicht wurden), begleitet von einer kurzen Zusammenfassung der Forschung in englischer Sprache (maximal 1 Seite). Die Bewerbungsfrist endet am 30. März 2024. Bitte senden Sie Ihre Bewerbung in elektronischer Form in einer einzigen PDF-Datei direkt an paulo.goncalves@usi.ch (auch Anfragen für weitere Informationen). Aufgrund der großen Anzahl eingegangener Bewerbungen werden nur Bewerber, deren Profile voraussichtlich den Anforderungen entsprechen, für weitere Details und eventuelle Vorstellungsgespräche kontaktiert. Wir werden qualifizierte Bewerber kurz nach Ablauf der Einreichungsfrist interviewen. Als eine Institution, die Vielfalt schätzt, ermutigt die USI insbesondere Frauen und alle Personen aus unterrepräsentierten Gruppen zur Bewerbung. Lugano, 4. März 2024 Offizielles Ausschreibungsdokument als PDF verfügbar.
requirements: Promotion in Systemdynamik, Simulationsmodellierung oder verwandtem Gebiet.
2 Jahre gleichwertige Forschungserfahrung.
Erfahrung mit Systemdynamikmodellen für öffentliche Gesundheit.
Erfahrung mit modellbasierten und datengesteuerten Politikanalysen.
Erfahrung in der Sammlung, Analyse und Verwaltung quantitativer Daten.
Erfahrung mit sozialen Engagementprozessen.
Ausgezeichnete Englischkenntnisse (mündlich und schriftlich).
Reisebereitschaft.

fr.title: Deux Chercheurs Postdoctoraux
fr.description: ## Description Poste ouvert à l'USI – Università della Svizzera italiana. Département/Institut : Faculté des sciences économiques – Institute of Management and Organisation (IMO). Rôle : Deux Chercheurs Postdoctoraux. Lieu : Lugano, Suisse (Canton du Tessin). 1 Institute of Management and Organization (IMO) Deux Chercheurs Postdoctoraux en Management à l'Institute of Management & Organization (IMO), Faculté d'Économie, à l'USI Università della Svizzera italiana. L'USI Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) est une université jeune et dynamique, un pôle d'opportunités ouvert sur le monde où les étudiants bénéficient d'une formation interdisciplinaire de qualité dans laquelle ils peuvent s'engager pleinement et être au centre de l'attention, et où nos chercheurs peuvent compter sur l'espace nécessaire pour poursuivre librement leur initiative. Fondée en 1996, l'USI est en constante évolution, relevant toujours de nouveaux défis tout en restant fidèle à ses trois principes directeurs : qualité, ouverture et responsabilité. Le directeur du Humanitarian Operations Group, Prof. Paulo Goncalves, invite à postuler pour deux postes de chercheur postdoctoral afin de travailler sur un projet EU-HORIZON de 4 ans visant à développer un Système Européen de Préparation aux Pandémies. ## Le Poste de Chercheur Postdoctoral Le chercheur postdoctoral travaillera sous la supervision scientifique du Prof. Paulo Goncalves (http://usi.to/iam). Les candidats retenus auront la possibilité de travailler au sein d'une équipe de recherche dynamique et dans un environnement scientifique multidisciplinaire et international. Les chercheurs postdoctoraux travailleront sur un projet EU-HORIZON visant à développer un Système Européen de Préparation aux Pandémies. Le projet est soutenu en Suisse par le Secrétariat d'État à la formation, à la recherche et à l'innovation (SERI). Les chercheurs postdoctoraux développeront un modèle de dynamique des systèmes pour évaluer la menace des urgences sanitaires et la capacité de réponse immédiate des différents pays, en identifiant les risques, en assurant la planification et en priorisant les activités de préparation aux urgences sanitaires. Ils devront également : - Collecter et nettoyer des données quantitatives européennes pertinentes pour le modèle dynamique. - Planifier et mener des activités d'engagement des parties prenantes, telles que des ateliers de modélisation participative des systèmes, des entretiens semi-structurés et des enquêtes. - Contribuer au développement du modèle en collaboration avec les partenaires de recherche. - Développer la structure du modèle SD, calibrer le modèle sur les données, et concevoir et tester des politiques de réponse sanitaire. - Concevoir et mettre en œuvre un simulateur de vol de gestion pour l'évaluation dynamique des menaces d'urgences sanitaires. - Publier dans des revues internationales de haute qualité évaluées par des pairs et présenter les résultats de la recherche lors de réunions nationales et internationales et de conférences scientifiques. - Suivre toute formation et/ou développement nécessaire. - Effectuer toutes les tâches administratives appropriées. ## Profil des Candidats Les candidats idéaux doivent satisfaire aux exigences suivantes : - Être titulaire d'un doctorat en dynamique des systèmes, modélisation de simulation ou dans un domaine connexe, et justifier de 2 ans d'expérience de recherche équivalente. - Expérience avec les modèles de dynamique des systèmes pour la santé publique - Expérience avec l'analyse de politiques basée sur des modèles et des données. - Expérience dans la collecte, l'analyse et la gestion de données quantitatives dans un environnement multidisciplinaire (par ex. économétrie, optimisation, simulation) - Expérience avec les processus d'engagement social (par ex. modélisation participative des systèmes, entretiens semi-structurés, ateliers, groupes de discussion). - Excellente maîtrise de l'anglais oral et écrit, langue officielle de la recherche ; la connaissance ou l'intérêt pour l'italien est apprécié. - Expérience de publication dans des revues internationales évaluées par des pairs. - Capacité à travailler de manière indépendante et autonome, à planifier, diriger et faire progresser sa propre recherche. - Fort intérêt personnel pour la santé publique et les modèles d'évaluation sanitaire - Volonté de voyager en Suisse et à l'étranger pour mener des recherches et assister à des conférences. ## Conditions du Contrat Les postes de recherche postdoctorale sont très compétitifs. Les décisions d'embauche sont basées sur le parcours, les intérêts, l'attitude et le potentiel de réussite académique du candidat. Le poste exige un engagement à temps plein et n'est pas compatible avec une activité professionnelle supplémentaire. Les candidats retenus travailleront sous la supervision du Professeur Gonçalves sur un projet EU-HORIZON visant à développer un Système Européen de Préparation aux Pandémies, financé pour 4 ans. Les chercheurs postdoctoraux auront la possibilité de collaborer et d'interagir avec une équipe d'autres doctorants et postdoctorants travaillant sur d'autres projets de recherche, ainsi qu'avec un réseau international de collaborateurs. Le lieu de travail est l'USI Università della Svizzera italiana, située à Lugano, en Suisse. La disponibilité pour voyager dans d'autres régions de Suisse et à l'étranger (à des fins de collaboration et de recherche) est requise. La date de début est dès que possible. ## La Candidature Les candidatures doivent contenir : (1) Une lettre de motivation personnelle dans laquelle le candidat décrit ses intérêts de recherche et sa motivation à postuler, (2) Un CV complet, incluant (i) la date de naissance, (ii) la nationalité, (iii) les noms de deux références (voir p. 3), et (iv) d'autres informations pertinentes pour le poste (par ex. modélisation de la dynamique des systèmes). (3) Les noms et coordonnées (par ex. e-mail et téléphone) de deux références professionnelles appuyant votre candidature. (4) Une version électronique des travaux de recherche (articles scientifiques publiés dans des revues internationales évaluées par des pairs), accompagnée d'un bref résumé de la recherche en anglais (1 page maximum). La date limite de candidature est le 30 mars 2024. Veuillez envoyer votre candidature sous forme électronique en un seul fichier PDF directement à paulo.goncalves@usi.ch (également pour toute demande d'information complémentaire). En raison du grand nombre de candidatures reçues, seuls les candidats dont le profil correspondra probablement aux postes seront contactés pour plus de détails et d'éventuels entretiens. Nous commencerons à interviewer les candidats qualifiés peu après la date limite de soumission. En tant qu'institution qui valorise la diversité, l'USI encourage particulièrement les candidatures des femmes et de toutes les personnes issues de groupes sous-représentés. Lugano, le 4 mars 2024 Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.
requirements: Doctorat en dynamique des systèmes, modélisation de simulation ou domaine connexe.
2 ans d'expérience de recherche équivalente.
Expérience avec les modèles de dynamique des systèmes pour la santé publique.
Expérience avec l'analyse de politiques basée sur des modèles et des données.
Expérience dans la collecte, l'analyse et la gestion de données quantitatives.
Expérience avec les processus d'engagement social.
Excellente maîtrise de l'anglais oral et écrit.
Disponibilité à voyager.

en.title: PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization
en.description: ## Descrizione Posizione aperta presso Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) and Università della Svizzera italiana (USI). Ruolo: PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization. Sede: Lugano, Svizzera (Canton Ticino). The Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) and the Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) are seeking applications for the position of PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization The Regenerative Medicine Division is part of the Institute for Translational Research, Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland. Strategic research areas of the Division are: in vitro disease modeling through biofabrication (e.g. age- related diseases, cancer metastases, musculo-skeletal diseases); design of novel technologies for drug screening; personalized medicine applications using human tissue biopsies. To promote the advancement of these research areas, the Regenerative Medicine Division combines microfluidics and microphysiological systems, 3D (bio)printing and computational simulations. We invite applications for a PhD position for conducting research in the project entitled “Enhancing human brain organoid complexity through biofabrication of a perfusable vasculature”. The project The Division has recently granted funding from the Swiss National Science Foundation through a collaboration between Prof. Simone Bersini and Prof. Arianna Baggiolini (Institute of Oncology Research). The 4 years project will deal with the biofabrication of miniaturized models of the human brain (i.e. organoids) and their integration with functional microvascular networks. This cutting-edge technology will allow to study key mechanisms of neural progenitor cell differentiation during brain development and to recapitulate increasingly complex architectures of the human brain. The project involves the biofabrication of 3D microvascular networks mimicking the blood brain barrier, their integration with high-throughput culture systems and the analysis of blood vessel-brain organoid interaction through multiple next-gen sequencing techniques and high-content screening. The Institute has access to cutting-edge facilities (e.g. bulk and single-cell RNAseq; spatial transcriptomics; mass spectrometry; confocal, multi-photon and electron microscopy) which are shared with the Institute for Research in Biomedicine and the Institute of Oncology Research within a dynamic, multidisciplinary and collaborative environment. The PhD Position The candidate will work under the scientific supervision of Prof. Simone Bersini (https://search.usi.ch/en/people/af37e00b27d5199e68bb294f19e50006/bersini-simone). The successful candidate will be offered the possibility to work in a dynamic research team and in a multidisciplinary and international scientific environment. He or she will be enrolled in the Doctoral Program in Biomedical Sciences at USI. The candidate will collaborate in the development of the institute research agenda. He or she will have shared responsibilities in the design, conduction and analysis of research projects in the field of biofabrication and molecular cell biology. In addition, the candidate is expected to present papers at scientific conferences and produce publications in high-ranking journals. ## Requisiti Candidates’ profile Ideal candidates should satisfy the following requirements: - A Master Degree in any Life Sciences or related disciplines, including Bioengineering. Priority given to Vascular Biology or Neuroscience background - High personal interest in organ-on-a-chip, biofabrication and (bio)-printing - Experience with (or strong commitment to quickly learn): 3D cell cultures (preferred experience 2 with vascular cells and/or brain organoids), imaging (confocal microscopy, preferably with 3D cultures), standard biological techniques (qPCR, western blot, elisa, immunofluorescence), microfabrication of microphysiological systems, analysis of datasets including those from omic technologies - Excellent skills in oral and written English - Self-motivation and exceptional commitment to experimental goals and deadlines - Strong organizational skills and ability to work independently as well as in a team - Critical data analysis and troubleshooting - Effectively communicate experimental data, maintain records and write protocols ## Cosa offriamo Contract terms The successful candidate will work as a PhD student in the Regenerative Medicine Division, and will have the possibility to interact with an international network of collaborators. Continuous and dynamic interactions with the lab of Prof. Baggiolini are expected to achieve the goals of the project. Workplace is BIOS+, located in Bellinzona, Switzerland. Availability to travel to other parts of Switzerland and abroad (for purposes of collaboration and research) is required. Starting date is March 1, 2026. The position will be kept open until a suitable candidate has been found. ## Candidatura The application Applications should contain: (1) a letter in which the applicants describe their research interests and the motivation to apply, (2) a complete CV, (3) copies of relevant diplomas and certificates, (4) a complete list of publications with details on the candidate’s contributions, (5) minimum 2 references (letters not required at this stage). Please send your application in electronic form or requests for further information to simone.bersini@eoc.ch (please use the following subject: PhD position-Vascularized brain) The conditions of appointment are those provided for by the Regulation concerning the hiring of EOC staff and doctors as well as, for the academic part, by the Statute and Regulations of USI academic staff. USI and the EOC aspire to be an exemplary university and employer and aim to ensure equality in the hiring, development, retention and promotion of staff, and that no one is disadvantaged on the basis of gender, culture, disability, sexual orientation or identity. As an institution that promotes diversity and inclusion, USI encourages applications from women and all people belonging to underrepresented categories. For further information, please contact: Prof. Simone Bersini, Institute for Translational Research (IRT), Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Phone +41 58 666 71 05). Lugano, November 19, 2025 The procedure in question is not subject to any means of appeal and/or remedy in law, since the contract to be concluded with the successful bidder is based on private law Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.

it.title: Dottorando in Biofabbricazione/Biologia Vascolare con focus sulla vascolarizzazione degli organoidi cerebrali
it.description: ## Descrizione Posizione aperta presso Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) e Università della Svizzera italiana (USI). Ruolo: Dottorando in Biofabbricazione/Biologia Vascolare con focus sulla vascolarizzazione degli organoidi cerebrali. Sede: Lugano, Svizzera (Canton Ticino). Continuous and dynamic interactions with the lab of Prof. Baggiolini are expected to achieve the goals of the project. Workplace is BIOS+, located in Bellinzona, Switzerland. Availability to travel to other parts of Switzerland and abroad (for purposes of collaboration and research) is required. Starting date is March 1, 2026. The position will be kept open until a suitable candidate has been found. ## Candidatura La candidatura The application Applications should contain: (1) a letter in which the applicants describe their research interests and the motivation to apply, (2) a complete CV, (3) copies of relevant diplomas and certificates, (4) a complete list of publications with details on the candidate’s contributions, (5) minimum 2 references (letters not required at this stage). Please send your application in electronic form or requests for further information to simone.bersini@eoc.ch (please use the following subject: PhD position-Vascularized brain) The conditions of appointment are those provided for by the Regulation concerning the hiring of EOC staff and doctors as well as, for the academic part, by the Statute and Regulations of USI academic staff. USI and the EOC aspire to be an exemplary university and employer and aim to ensure equality in the hiring, development, retention and promotion of staff, and that no one is disadvantaged on the basis of gender, culture, disability, sexual orientation or identity. As an institution that promotes diversity and inclusion, USI encourages applications from women and all people belonging to underrepresented categories. For further information, please contact: Prof. Simone Bersini, Institute for Translational Research (IRT), Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Phone +41 58 666 71 05). Lugano, November 19, 2025 The procedure in question is not subject to any means of appeal and/or remedy in law, since the contract to be concluded with the successful bidder is based on private law Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.

de.title: PhD-Student Biofabrikation/Vaskuläre Biologie (Vaskularisierung von Gehirnorganoiden)
de.description: ## Beschreibung Stelle offen am Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) und der Università della Svizzera italiana (USI). Rolle: PhD-Student in Biofabrikation/Vaskulärer Biologie mit Schwerpunkt auf Vaskularisierung von Gehirnorganoiden. Standort: Lugano, Schweiz (Kanton Tessin). The Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC) and the Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) are seeking applications for the position of PhD student in Biofabrication/Vascular Biology with a focus on brain organoid vascularization The Regenerative Medicine Division is part of the Institute for Translational Research, Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland. Strategic research areas of the Division are: in vitro disease modeling through biofabrication (e.g. age- related diseases, cancer metastases, musculo-skeletal diseases); design of novel technologies for drug screening; personalized medicine applications using human tissue biopsies. To promote the advancement of these research areas, the Regenerative Medicine Division combines microfluidics and microphysiological systems, 3D (bio)printing and computational simulations. We invite applications for a PhD position for conducting research in the project entitled “Enhancing human brain organoid complexity through biofabrication of a perfusable vasculature”. Das Projekt The Division has recently granted funding from the Swiss National Science Foundation through a collaboration between Prof. Simone Bersini and Prof. Arianna Baggiolini (Institute of Oncology Research). The 4 years project will deal with the biofabrication of miniaturized models of the human brain (i.e. organoids) and their integration with functional microvascular networks. This cutting-edge technology will allow to study key mechanisms of neural progenitor cell differentiation during brain development and to recapitulate increasingly complex architectures of the human brain. The project involves the biofabrication of 3D microvascular networks mimicking the blood brain barrier, their integration with high-throughput culture systems and the analysis of blood vessel-brain organoid interaction through multiple next-gen sequencing techniques and high-content screening. The Institute has access to cutting-edge facilities (e.g. bulk and single-cell RNAseq; spatial transcriptomics; mass spectrometry; confocal, multi-photon and electron microscopy) which are shared with the Institute for Research in Biomedicine and the Institute of Oncology Research within a dynamic, multidisciplinary and collaborative environment. Die PhD-Stelle The candidate will work under the scientific supervision of Prof. Simone Bersini (https://search.usi.ch/en/people/af37e00b27d5199e68bb294f19e50006/bersini-simone). The successful candidate will be offered the possibility to work in a dynamic research team and in a multidisciplinary and international scientific environment. He or she will be enrolled in the Doctoral Program in Biomedical Sciences at USI. The candidate will collaborate in the development of the institute research agenda. He or she will have shared responsibilities in the design, conduction and analysis of research projects in the field of biofabrication and molecular cell biology. In addition, the candidate is expected to present papers at scientific conferences and produce publications in high-ranking journals. ## Anforderungen Profil des Kandidaten Ideal candidates should satisfy the following requirements: - A Master Degree in any Life Sciences or related disciplines, including Bioengineering. Priority given to Vascular Biology or Neuroscience background - High personal interest in organ-on-a-chip, biofabrication and (bio)-printing - Experience with (or strong commitment to quickly learn): 3D cell cultures (preferred experience 2 with vascular cells and/or brain organoids), imaging (confocal microscopy, preferably with 3D cultures), standard biological techniques (qPCR, western blot, elisa, immunofluorescence), microfabrication of microphysiological systems, analysis of datasets including those from omic technologies - Excellent skills in oral and written English - Self-motivation and exceptional commitment to experimental goals and deadlines - Strong organizational skills and ability to work independently as well as in a team - Critical data analysis and troubleshooting - Effectively communicate experimental data, maintain records and write protocols ## Was wir bieten Vertragsbedingungen The successful candidate will work as a PhD student in the Regenerative Medicine Division, and will have the possibility to interact with an international network of collaborators. Continuous and dynamic interactions with the lab of Prof. Baggiolini are expected to achieve the goals of the project. Workplace is BIOS+, located in Bellinzona, Switzerland. Availability to travel to other parts of Switzerland and abroad (for purposes of collaboration and research) is required. Starting date is March 1, 2026. The position will be kept open until a suitable candidate has been found. ## Bewerbung Die Bewerbung The application Applications should contain: (1) a letter in which the applicants describe their research interests and the motivation to apply, (2) a complete CV, (3) copies of relevant diplomas and certificates, (4) a complete list of publications with details on the candidate’s contributions, (5) minimum 2 references (letters not required at this stage). Please send your application in electronic form or requests for further information to simone.bersini@eoc.ch (please use the following subject: PhD position-Vascularized brain) The conditions of appointment are those provided for by the Regulation concerning the hiring of EOC staff and doctors as well as, for the academic part, by the Statute and Regulations of USI academic staff. USI and the EOC aspire to be an exemplary university and employer and aim to ensure equality in the hiring, development, retention and promotion of staff, and that no one is disadvantaged on the basis of gender, culture, disability, sexual orientation or identity. As an institution that promotes diversity and inclusion, USI encourages applications from women and all people belonging to underrepresented categories. For further information, please contact: Prof. Simone Bersini, Institute for Translational Research (IRT), Faculty of Biomedical Sciences, Università della Svizzera italiana (USI) and Ente Ospedaliero Cantonale (EOC), Switzerland (simone.bersini@eoc.ch – Phone +41 58 666 71 05). Lugano, November 19, 2025 The procedure in question is not subject to any means of appeal and/or remedy in law, since the contract to be concluded with the successful bidder is based on private law Bando ufficiale disponibile in PDF.

Kritische Datenanalyse und Fehlersuche.
Effektive Kommunikation experimenteller Daten, Führung von Aufzeichnungen und Verfassen von Protokollen.
